_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning strategies for building and managing effective teams, and how to evaluate how well a team is collaborating and recommend improvements.

_source.w: We are learning strategies for building and managing effective teams, and how to evaluate how well a team is collaborating and recommend improvements.
y: The ability to construct, manage and evaluate teams is a leadership skill that is valued in every field and profession.
Learning to give and receive constructive feedback about collaboration helps teams grow stronger and produce better work.
These skills prepare us for collaborative environments in senior school, university, the workplace and community organisations.
eg: We reflect on each group member's contribution to a completed task and provide constructive feedback, demonstrating sensitivity to different perspectives.
We explain how clarity about roles contributes to group success and develop strategies for aligning role descriptions with both task demands and team members' skills.
We evaluate a recent team experience using specific criteria — such as communication, task distribution, conflict resolution and outcomes — and make recommendations for improvement.
We research effective team management strategies used in professional settings and discuss which ones we could apply to our own group work.
We design a team charter before starting a major project, outlining goals, roles, expectations and processes for resolving disagreements.
We discuss what makes some teams highly effective while others struggle, drawing on examples from sport, business, science and the arts.
